hook: You already know how to compute $E[X]$ for a single random variable, and you've seen how conditioning on an event can sharpen your predictions. But what happens when the information you condition on is itself random — changing with every outcome? This unit gives you the tools to handle exactly that, turning partial information into a powerful engine for computing expectations and variances.
concept: The unifying idea is that conditioning on a random variable $Y$ doesn't just give you a number — it gives you a new random variable that captures how your best prediction of $X$ updates as $Y$ varies, formally expressed through the tower property $E[E[X|Y]] = E[X]$.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to break a complex expectation into manageable conditional pieces using $E[X] = E[E[X|Y]]$, making seemingly intractable problems straightforward.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Conditional Expectation E[X|Y] as a Random Variable
topic_roadmap.description: Master the conceptual leap of viewing $E[X|Y]$ not as a fixed number but as a function of the random variable $Y$, and work fluently with its properties.
Apply the decomposition $\text{Var}(X) = E[\text{Var}(X|Y)] + \text{Var}(E[X|Y])$ to separate the sources of randomness in $X$ and compute variances in multi-stage probability models.
